title: Albert Morot 2015 Les Teurons Premier Cru  (Beaune)

description: A structured wine that is also full of ripe fruit. Its red-berry fruits are encased in the tannins that still need to soften. The wine will be generous, full of berry flavors and with a dense texture. Drink this big wine from 2024.
title: Jean-Claude Boisset 2015 Les Grèves Premier Cru  (Beaune)

description: Subdued in oak, this bright intriguing wine does a nice job showcasing tart tangy flavors of green olive and green apple, while still maintaining a freshness at its core. It is moderately structured and teased in a twist of lime.
title: MacRostie 2015 Sangiacomo Vineyard Chardonnay (Carneros)

description: This is a lush, elegant example of Condrieu. It's broad, but with bright, balanced acids that point up notes of honeyed peaches, apricots and pineapple. Drink now–2018.
title: Roland Grangier 2015 Les Terrasses  (Condrieu)

description: This premier cru is on the cooler left bank of the Serein river, making this vintage a crisply mineral wine that's tight with zesty lemon and fine crisp acidity. The hints of yellow fruits are still to develop. The wine is structured and needs to age, so drink from 2018.
title: Jean-Marc Brocard 2015 Vau de Vey Premier Cru  (Chablis)

description: The top wine from this estate, this is also the most dense and tight. An ageworthy medley of red fruits and peach is sharpened by acidity and a peppery edge. It does have a lighter touch with its poised crisp texture and its juicy aftertaste. However, it will be even better from the end of 2016.
title: Château Minuty 2015 Rose et Or Rosé (Côtes de Provence)

description: Ripe and spicy, this is an obviously wood-aged wine. Spice and toastiness still dominate, although in a few months the potential of the juicy black fruits should become apparent. Wait to drink this wine until 2018.
title: Quevedo 2015 Oscar's Red Red (Douro)

description: Subtitled Vin de Vinarchiste, this is a blended wine from organically grown grapes which include the local Fer and Périgord alongside Bordeaux varieties. It is a crisp wine showing some tight, almost green-pepper flavors as well as smoky acidity. It is young but will be ready to drink soon. Drink from early 2017.
title: Château Tour des Gendres 2015 La Vigne d'Albert Red (Bergerac)

description: Basic apple, melon and bready aromas are fine but not distinct or complex. Ditto the palate, which feels balanced but highly basic. Simple papaya and melon flavors finish flat.
title: Chilensis 2015 Reserva Estate Bottled Chardonnay (Maule Valley)

description: Gritty citrus aromas are oily but adequate. This basic SB tastes of citrus and green vegetables. Beyond that, it's mostly fresh in feel.
title: Santa Luz 2015 Alba Sauvignon Blanc (Central Valley)

description: A touch of promising reduction still hovers on the nose. The palate is both firm and fruity. This wine is gorgeously ripe, with aromatic fruit captured at the perfect moment. It has grip and muscle, along with juicy, firm freshness that lets the dark and almost brooding black cherry, blueberry and blackberry fruit speak. On the finish, peppery spice appears amid the berries and bright freshness.
title: Markowitsch 2015 Blaufränkisch (Carnuntum)

description: While intensely mineral on the nose, wafting of struck flint and crushed slate, this plucky, light-footed Riesling boasts a decadently juicy, fruity palate. Honeyed tones of peach and nectarine are sumptuously sweet yet balanced neatly by a piercing strike of acidity. The finish is lavishly persistent.
title: Thanisch 2015 Lieserer Niederberg Helden Spätlese Riesling (Mosel)

description: This is a gentle and easy-going Chardonnay. It is soft, lightly buttery and conveys apricot and ripe pear flavors. The aftertaste just touches on acidity.
title: Café du Midi 2015 Chardonnay (Vin de France)

description: A lovely lift of lemon oil on the nose gives a seductive headiness to the wine. The palate then convinces with generous orange flesh and peel, with tangerine spice and juicy fulsomeness, but never without citric zesty tension. This has drive, freshness and a lively energy. You simply want to drown in this. Lovely now, but built to last. Drink now through 2040.
title: Malat 2015 Steinbühel Riesling (Kremstal)

description: Delicate aromas suggesting acacia flower, chamomile, yellow stone fruit and a whiff of hazelnut set the tone on this medium-bodied white. The ripe succulent palate doles out mature yellow apple, nectarine zest and a hint of honey alongside fresh acidity. A note of bitter almond marks the finish.
title: Panizzi 2015  Vernaccia di San Gimignano

description: Dry and snappy but also with a little depth, this nicely balanced wine made from Pinot Noir shows light flavors like watermelon and canteloupe. The texture is appetizingly crisp and the body rather light.
title: Navarro 2015 Rosé (Anderson Valley)

description: Hints of honeysuckle are very restrained on the nose. The palate is dry and shaped by rounded lemony freshness and hints of zesty spice. This is wonderfully light and deeply aromatic, but not obviously so. A wonderfully subtle expression of Muscat.
title: Allimant-Laugner 2015 Muscat (Alsace)

description: Citrus aromas reveal this wine's essentially crisp character. Plenty of acidity balances the rich white fruits while the mineral texture adds bite to this well-made wine. It will be ready to drink from 2019.
title: Domaines Devillard 2015 Domaine de la Garenne  (Pouilly-Fuissé)

description: Firmly tannic and structured this is a young wine that will need time to soften. It has the foursquare character that is often found in Nuits Saint-Georges. The fruit weight is there, promising a ripe red-fruit future. The wine should be ready from 2020.
title: L. Tramier & Fils 2015 Laurent Dufouleur  (Nuits-St.-Georges)

description: This has a shy nose, offering subtle whiffs of white stone fruit and spring wildflower while the simple palate offers orchard fruit, citrus zest and bitter almond flavors. It's fresh, with zesty acidity.
title: Tenuta dei Mille 2015 Anita Grillo (Terre Siciliane)

description: Punchy raspberry and red-plum aromas are light and airy. That also describes the palate, which is dilute and lacks stuffing. Flavors of raspberry Kool-Aid and mild oak are fresh and floral on the finish. This may do best as a chilled simple sipper.
title: Bodegas Escudero 2015 Solar de Becquer  (Rioja)

description: This is a GSM rosé, loaded with watermelon and strawberry fruit, and sporting a slightly dusty mouthfeel. The persistent finish and texture keep it interesting all the way through.
title: Quady North 2015 Rosé (Rogue Valley)

description: Crafted in a New World way, this displays toasty, creamy oak and baked apple aromas. The palate is healthy and bold, while flavors of popcorn, white bread, butter, honeydew and banana maintain freshness and clarity across the finish.
title: Viña Cobos 2015 Felino Chardonnay (Mendoza)

description: Fresh earth and gloriously ripe stone fruit make for a convincing opening on the nose. The palate swings with this sonorous, earthy edge and frames rich, ripe and rounded pear and yellow plum fruit. Overall, there is zesty fresh citrus that gathers everything together and presents a concentrated wine with drive and personality. The finish is long, clean and convincing. Drink 2020–2040.
title: Pichler-Krutzler 2015 Loibenberg Riesling (Wachau)

description: Pale and refreshing, this is lightly scented by orange blossoms and strawberries, with similar flavors following. It's a splendid sipping wine, with plenty of acid to buoy up the light fruit.
title: Portlandia 2015 Pinot Noir Rosé (Willamette Valley)

description: Savory oak aromas and related spice notes blend with earthy berry scents. This wine has a racy mouthfeel tempered by creamy oak. Woody, spicy, earthy flavors of berry fruits and tomato conclude with a note of vanilla on a traditional Rioja-style finish.
title: Vallobera 2015 Reserva  (Rioja)

description: Strong flavors of clove and mint dominate this unusual Riesling. It's quite dry, with good penetration, though a world apart from most of its peers. With the right food (Thai?), the potent flavors might prove complementary.
title: Kudos 2015 Riesling (Willamette Valley)

description: Called Vermentinu in Corsica, the grape gives an aromatic wine with wildflower scents. On the palate, it's crisp with pears and red apples that are cut by the squeeze of citrus. Drink this attractive wine now.
title: Clos Venturi 2015 1769 Vermentino (Corse)

description: While hints of earth and bramble on the nose seem a touch savory, the palate of this concentrated semi-dry wine is richly flavored with fresh peach, orange and apricot. It's juicy and fresh, accented by soft orange acidity and a brisk finish. Drink now through 2018 to enjoy its fruity vitality.
title: Barnstormer 2015 Semi-Dry Riesling (Finger Lakes)

description: Fresh red apples waft from nose to palate on this semidry Riesling. It's vivaciously tart but concentrated in fresh lemon, lime and tangerine flavors. The finish is moderately long but delightfully honeyed.
title: Standing Stone 2015 Riesling (Finger Lakes)

description: This bottling, which includes 20% Sémillon, offers aromas of cut grass, sliced Anjou pear and a lemon-lime freshness on the nose. Sour Granny Smith apple and green lime rinds rule the palate, making for a solid and classic style of Sauv Blanc.
title: Refugio Ranch 2015 Tiradora Sauvignon Blanc (Santa Ynez Valley)

description: Hints of aromatic orange peel and white summer blossom add a certain exotic headiness to an earthy white blend from Burgenland. The palate is slender but laden with appetizing fruit flavours on surprisingly light, delicate feet—the result of sunny summer days and cool nights. Drink this soon to make most of those enticing, primary fruit notes and lasting aromas.
title: Meinklang 2015 Burgenlandweiß White (Burgenland)

description: This smooth, ripe wine comes from organic grapes grown in clay and stone soil that adds a mineral edge. Spice balances with citrus and red-plum fruits to give a lively wine with a perfumed aftertaste.
title: Château la Mascaronne 2015 Quat'Saisons Rosé (Côtes de Provence)

description: This wine is fat, fruity and creamy, with just a touch of minerality. Its ripe fullness lends plenty of depth and concentration, within a lightly structured frame. Drink from 2018.
title: Simonnet-Febvre 2015 Vaillons Premier Cru  (Chablis)

description: Wood aromas and flavors are happily balanced by the ripe berry fruits. So the firm structure is lifted by juicy acidity and fruit to give a wine that will be spicy, fruity and succulent. Drink from 2018.
title: Château Merlet 2015  Bordeaux

description: Smooth and simple, this wine offers aromas of citrus pith, cantaloupe and green apple. Flavors suggest honeydew and tart apple juice.
title: Domeniile Sahateni 2015 Nomad Dealurie Munteniei Chardonnay (Dealu Mare)

description: This producer is the only grower of Caladoc grapes in Portugal, and one of the few in the world. Blended here with Tinta Roriz, the grape gives a ripe, soft and perfumed wine. It has a generous character, all fruit and balanced acidity. Drink now.
title: DFJ Vinhos 2015 Paxis Red (Lisboa)

description: An herbal wine with gooseberry and citrus flavors, this is full of fruitiness with acidity and touch of flint and steel in the texture. The wine is ready to drink.
title: Pierre Jacolin 2015 Domaine du Manay  (Menetou-Salon)

description: Attractively herbaceous, this is a crisp, light and bright wine. Tight green fruits and plenty of acidity give it a juicy character that makes it refreshing. Drink now. This is one of 15 Bordeaux chateaus by Crus and Domaines de France.
title: Château Mezain 2015  Bordeaux Blanc

description: Grown at 1,800 feet, the vines for this wine benefit from the cool conditions needed to develop aromatic intensity. With crisp acidity combined with spiced pear and nutmeg flavors, it's both tightly textured and very fruity. Drink now.
title: Colinas do Douro 2015 Superior White (Douro)

description: Spry and sheer, this exceptionally light-bodied Kabinett offers piercing tangerine flavor with ethereal flair. Lemon-lime acidity enlivens the midpalate, carrying though to the zesty, electric finish. Drink now through 2020.
designation: Serrig Schloss Saarsteiner Kabinett Grosse Lage
title: Schloss Saarstein 2015 Serrig Schloss Saarsteiner Kabinett Grosse Lage Riesling (Mosel)

description: This well-made dry Furmint has a complex bouquet of citrus flowers, apple blossom and freshly cut white peach. In the mouth, there are flavors of peach, pineapple and passion fruit that dance on your tongue before the crisp acidic finish.
title: Gróf Degenfeld 2015 Zomborka Furmint (Tokaj)

description: This SB hails from Huasco, Chile's great northern frontier. It offers aromas of celery, white pepper and cool citrus. The mouthfeel is linear, maybe a touch monotone, while the palate and finish are made up of salty, briny citrus flavors and cutting acidity.
title: Ventisquero 2015 Kalfu Sumpai Sauvignon Blanc (Chile)

description: This blend of Sauvignon Blanc and Moschofilero offers floral aromas of rose, lemon blossom and a touch of spice. On the palate, the wine is focused with a floral flair. Tropical fruit, citrus and rose are layered but the wine finishes clean and with pep.
title: Semeli 2015 Mountain Sun White (Peloponnese)

description: The aromas seem to lack clarity, with white flower and orange rind notes struggling to get to the surface. It drinks medium sweet with abundant floral and orange-peel flavors that provide appeal.
title: Snoqualmie 2015 Winemaker's Select Riesling (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: The Borba cooperative is in one of the most productive Alentejo wine regions. With this simple and fruity wine, it has captured warmth as well as ripe tannins. Berry fruits and acidity give it a lift. Drink now.
title: Adega Cooperativa de Borba 2015 Brado Red (Alentejano)

description: Distinct yet unusual aromas of vanilla extract and butterscotch announce and confirm that this was barrel fermented. Low vibrancy and bounce on the palate create a sense of flat heaviness. Salty peach and melon flavors are buried in wood spice, while this tastes dry, pithy and resiny on a mild finish.
region_1: Vino de la Tierra de Castilla
title: Finca Constancia 2015 Parcela 52 Verdejo (Vino de la Tierra de Castilla)

description: This wine's spring blossom, yellow apple and almond scents carry over to the light, rather diluted palate. Although simple, it's a great utility pour for large, informal gatherings or picnics.
title: I Campi 2015 Campo Base  (Soave)

description: This fresh, enjoyable white has passion fruit, citrus and saline aromas and flavors. It's simple, with a clean, fresh finish.
title: Assuli 2015 Fiordiligi Grillo (Terre Siciliane)

description: Régnié, is the newest of the Beaujolais cru village appellations. The grapes come from vines owned by the chateau, giving a wine that is all about ripe fruit and some firm tannins. It is still developing and will be better from 2017.
title: Château de Pizay 2015  Régnié

description: Exuberantly fruity, this full-bodied wine smells and tastes like black-cherry juice, and is nicely dry on the palate. Good fruit acidity and moderate tannins balance all the fruit concentration and high alcohol level.
title: Portalupi 2015 Shake Ridge Ranch Barbera (Amador County)

description: There is a toasty character to this wine with its (not unpleasant) lightly oxidized flavors. There is also perfumed fruit and flavors of ripe rich fruitiness. It is a finely structured wine, ready to drink now.
title: Casal da Coelheira 2015 Private Collection Branco Verdelho (Tejo)

description: Electric acidity penetrates through this sumptuously fruity wine full of rich white-peach and grapefruit flavors. Vibrantly fresh and floral, it's sharpened by a razor's edge of minerality that lingers through the finish. It's gorgeous now for its ebullience and youth but should improve well into the 2020's.
title: Balthasar Ress 2015 Schloss Reichartshausen Spätlese Riesling (Rheingau)

description: Hints of buttered toast and roasted nuts mark the nose of this medium-bodied wine. It's plump on the palate, blending cinnamon, clove and cashew notes with melon, peach and pineapple fruit, then comes to a close in more oak spice and some leesy richness.
title: Henschke 2015 Croft Chardonnay (Adelaide Hills)

description: This is a delicious, ripe and fruity wine that comes from the south of Beaujolais. It has ripe red-berry fruits and soft tannins allied to a juicy acidity that keeps it all fresh. Drink now.
title: Domaine des Terres Dorées 2015 L'Ancien  (Beaujolais)
